displayMetadata.name: PREPARATION
displayMetadata.value:
1. Measure rice using the measuring cup
2. Rinse with tap water and leave to soak for approximately 20 minutes
3. Drain off excess water. To cook, add water to your preference and turn on the rice cooker
4. After cooking, allow rice to stand for about 15 minutes
5. Fluff or stir rice with a serving spoon. Let the steam evaporate

metaData.Preparation:
• Sushi Rice Rice:
1. After washing the Japonica rice (3 cups), soak it in water for 30 minutes before cooking
2. Cook in rice cooker and leave it for 15 minutes after the rice is cooked
3. Prepare sushi vinegar by mixing rice vinegar (3 1/4 cups), sugar (3 tablespoons) and salt (1 teaspoon) in a sauce pan
4. Put the pan on low heat until the sugar and salt dissolves and cool the vinegar mixture
5. Spread the hot steamed rice into a large non-metallic plate or bowl (Best to use a wooden bowl to help the rice to cool quickly)
6.Sprinkle the vinegar mixture over the rice and fold the rice with a spatula or wooden spoon quickly
7. Store the sushi rice at room temperature and cover with a damp cloth
8. Ready to serve

metaData.Key Information: •Please note that the quality of vacuum-packed rice remains unaffected should the packaging not arrive fully vacuum-sealed.
metaData.Preparation:
• Measuring the rice:
Measure the rice accurately using a measuring cup
•. Rinse free rice is ready to cook without rinsing:
You don't need to rinse the rice with tap water before cooking
• Mineral water:
For tastier rice, use mineral water instead of tap water
• Measuring the water:
1. Use a little more water than usual (about 5%)
2. If the rice cooker has markings specifically for rinse free rice, add water to the level indicated
• How to cook rice:
1. Place the appropriate amount of rice in the rice cooker
2. Make sure your cooker is in "white rice cooking" mode and turn it on
3. Leave the rice in water for approximately 30 minutes before cooking unless your rice cooker has a microcomputer function
• After cooking:
Let the rice stand for about 15 minutes after cooking
• Fluffing:
1. Stir the rice with a serving spoon from the bottom up to "unstick" the grains and fluff
2. Let the steam evaporate
metaData.Storage Information:
• Directions:
1. Keep this bag away from babies and children
2. Store in a cool place or refrigerate after opening
• Warning:
1. Placement over the head or face may result in suffocation
2. This bag is flammable, never put it close to a fire

metaData.Preparation:
Directions:
1. Quickly rinse at first, then rinse lightly for 4 to 5 times.
2. Pour water according to your rice cooker pot scale, and also can be adjusted on your preference.
3. Soak rice for 30 to 60 minutes.
4. Cook rice according to your rice cooker manual.
5. Let the rice steam for 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Lightly loosen up the whole rice, bringing up the rice from the bottom of the pot, and let the excess moisture to evaporate.
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool and dry place

metaData.Key Information: Iwate Furusato Hitomebore, also known as Furusato-mai, is supplied by JA Iwate Furusato Agricultural Cooperative Association (Hereinafter JA Iwate). It is highly valued not only for its taste, but also for its safety and environmental friendliness. Hitomebore is a crossbred rice having Koshihikari as a mother, and Hatsuboshi as a father. This glossy rice also has a name which means Love at first sight, and is currently the second largest production after Koshihikari in Japan.<<<<<
metaData.Preparation:
Directions:
1. Wash rice quickly with full water by hand, and change water right after washing. Rinse and discard water 3~4 times until it gets clear.
2. Pour the amount water as per a rice cooker and also can be adjusted on your preference.
3. Soak rice in water for one hour. Soaking time may be longer in cold water.
4. Switch on a rice cooker.
5. Keep a rice cooker for 15 minutes with a lid on.
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool and dry place

metaData.Key Information:
* Please note that this product is not originally vacuum-sealed.
Koshihikari is one of the most, if not the most popular Japanese short-grain rice known for its sweet taste and slight stickiness. It is harvested from the Niigata Prefecture. This variety of rice accounts for almost 40% of rice production in Japan. Musenmai means no-wash rice. Due to modern japanese technology, the traditional process of washing japanese rice to remove the hada nuka or skin bran, is no longer necessary.
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool and dry place

* Please note that this product is not originally vacuum-sealed.
Akitakomachi is one out of the two most popular variety of japanese rice grain in Japan, apart from Koshihikari. It is harvested from the Akita prefecture. It is similar to Koshihikari but of a slightly less sticky texture. This rice grain is especially suited for used in japanese food such as sushi, mochi, as well as onigiri. Musenmai means no-wash rice. Due to modern japanese technology, the traditional process of washing japanese rice to remove the hada nuka or skin bran, is no longer necessary.

metaData.Key Information:
* Please note that this product is not originally vacuum-sealed.
Yumepirika was developed by the researchers at Hokkaido prefectural government's Kamikawa Agricultural Experiment Station in Bippu. Although Hokkaido was a region that many people consider unsuited for rice cultivation due to its cold climate, researchers used cold snow water to prevent the breeding of varmints and minimise use of chemicals in the process. This subsequently led to the grain's distribution nationwide, followed by receiving the top "Special A" ranking conferred by the Japan Grain Inspection Association. This rice is very soft, sticky and sweet, thus earning its place as a premium quality rice of Japan. It is recommended to be eaten on its own. Oxygen absorber packets help to preserve freshness of rice and prevent growth of pests, with no side effects.
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool and dry place

offers.description: Buy 1 Hokkaido Yumepirika Japanese Rice @ $5.80 Off

metaData.Key Information: A new brand of Hokkaido rice debuted in 2020. YUKI, which means snow in Japanese, represents its whiteness when cooked, and each grain of rice is big and beautiful. It is characterised by a slightly stiff and firm texture, being of high amylose and low protein content. The rice is therefore less sticky and has a lighter flavor. The production gives a higher crop yield compared to other Hokkaido rice like Nanatsuboshi and so offers a more affordable price to customers. This rice is highly unique since it is originally produced by Matsubara Beikoku Co., Ltd. and the contracted farmers in Hokkaido who are passionate in cultivating rice. Oxygen absorber packets help to preserve freshness of rice and prevent growth of pests, with no side effects.<<
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool and dry place
